site.name: Forma Demo — ChatGPT Build
site.description: A landing page proving Forma turns structured manifests into coherent static websites.

index.title: Forma — Structured Manifests to Polished Sites
index.description: Forma compiles structured site definitions into polished static sites using reusable blocks, strict tokens, and theme-safe rendering.

blocks.title: Structured manifests in. Polished sites out.
blocks.subtitle: Forma compiles site.json manifests into production-ready static HTML using reusable blocks, strict semantic tokens, and theme-safe rendering. No hallucinated markup. No design drift.

blocks.problem_1: Agents hallucinate class names that don't exist in any stylesheet
blocks.problem_2: Design drift — page 1 looks different from page 5, nothing matches
blocks.problem_3: Two agents on the same site lose each other's decisions
blocks.problem_4: No validation — broken output ships to production silently
blocks.solution_1: 32 pre-built blocks — agents select, never write markup
blocks.solution_2: 31 semantic tokens enforce visual consistency across every page
blocks.solution_3: One manifest coordinates all agents without shared context
blocks.solution_4: Forma Judge validates structure AND intent before shipping

blocks.title: Manifest to production in 4 steps
blocks.step_1_desc: One site.json file defines pages, blocks, content, theme, goal, and audience. This is the single source of truth.
blocks.step_2_desc: Choose from 32 production blocks across 8 categories. Each block is token-based, responsive, and theme-agnostic.
blocks.step_3_desc: The compiler reads the manifest, pulls blocks, injects content, applies theme tokens. Static HTML out. One command.
blocks.step_4_desc: Forma Judge checks every page against schema and goal-based rules. If it passes, it ships. If not, it tells you exactly why.

blocks.title: This is how agents describe a website
blocks.subtitle: No HTML. No CSS. Just structured intent. The compiler handles the rest.

blocks.section_title: Every pattern a landing page needs
blocks.section_subtitle: Conversion, trust, features, process, forms, media. Agents pick blocks — they never write markup.
blocks.feature_1_description: comparison, conversion-panel, problem-solution, faq, guarantee, pricing, cta — every decision-stage pattern.
blocks.feature_2_description: trust-bar, logo-wall, testimonials, case-study, stats — proof that builds confidence.
blocks.feature_3_description: feature-grid, feature-spotlight, bento-grid, system-diagram — product storytelling at every depth.
blocks.feature_4_description: process, pipeline — step-by-step flows with timelines and connecting lines.
blocks.feature_5_description: contact-form, lead-magnet — capture and convert with token-styled inputs.
blocks.feature_6_description: video-embed, before-after, code-preview, changelog — show the product, show the proof.

blocks.section_title: Raw AI output vs Forma output
What agents said while using Forma
blocks.testimonial_1_quote: The token contract caught 3 missing variables before I even ran the build. Without it, those would have shipped as invisible bugs.
blocks.testimonial_2_quote: I validated the manifest against the schema, ran the judge, and every page passed 28/28 checks. No manual review needed.
blocks.testimonial_3_quote: Switching from Graphite to Bone and Ink was one line in the manifest. Every block re-themed automatically.

blocks.legal_text: 2026 Forma. MIT License. Built by agents, for agents.
